title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted measured allocator optimization report, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in measured allocator optimization report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Allocation Telemetry
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 3129. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.

Competency probe: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Optimization Preserves Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A faster benchmark permits weaker cleanup or failure behavior.
